questions.q: The Dashavatara are the ten principal incarnations of which deity?
questions.explanation: According to the Puranas, the Dashavatara are the ten principal descents of Vishnu to restore dharma.

questions.q: What does the word 'Dashavatara' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Dasha' means ten and 'avatara' means descent, so Dashavatara denotes the ten incarnations of Vishnu.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu takes the form of a fish in the Dashavatara sequence?
questions.explanation: Matsya, the fish avatar, is the first of the Dashavatara and saves King Manu from the great deluge.

questions.q: In the classical Dashavatara list, which avatar of Vishnu appears as a tortoise?
questions.explanation: Kurma, the tortoise avatar, supports Mount Mandara on his back during the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu takes the form of a boar to rescue the earth?
questions.explanation: Varaha, the boar avatar, lifts the earth goddess Bhudevi from the cosmic waters in the Puranic accounts.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu has the form of a man-lion?
questions.explanation: Narasimha, the man-lion avatar, appears to destroy the demon king Hiranyakashipu in the Puranas.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is the hero of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Rama, the seventh avatar, is the prince of Ayodhya whose story is told in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu delivers the Bhagavad Gita in the Mahabharata?
questions.explanation: Krishna, often counted as the eighth avatar, speaks the Bhagavad Gita to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is prophesied to appear in the future at the end of Kali Yuga?
questions.explanation: Kalki is the tenth and future avatar, prophesied in the Puranas to appear at the end of Kali Yuga.

questions.q: Which emblem, a spinning discus, is most associated with Vishnu of the Dashavatara?
questions.explanation: The Sudarshana Chakra, a spinning discus, is the signature weapon of Vishnu across his incarnations.

questions.q: In the classical Dashavatara sequence, which avatar of Vishnu comes first?
questions.explanation: The classical sequence opens with Matsya, the fish, followed by Kurma, the tortoise.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu appears as a dwarf Brahmin who measures the cosmos in three strides?
questions.explanation: Vamana, the dwarf avatar, requests three paces of land from King Bali and then covers all worlds in three strides.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is depicted as a warrior-sage wielding an axe?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, the sixth avatar, is the Brahmin warrior who bears an axe (parashu) given to him by Shiva.

questions.q: From which demon king does the Narasimha avatar of Vishnu protect the devotee Prahlada?
questions.explanation: Narasimha destroys the demon king Hiranyakashipu, the father of the devoted Prahlada, in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: From which king does the Vamana avatar of Vishnu request three paces of land?
questions.explanation: Vamana approaches the generous King Mahabali and asks for three paces of land, which he uses to span the worlds.

questions.q: During which cosmic event does the Kurma avatar of Vishnu serve as a pivot beneath Mount Mandara?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: Kurma supports Mount Mandara during the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk, in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the Matsya avatar of Vishnu rescues which figure from the great flood?
questions.explanation: Matsya guides the boat of Manu and the seeds of life safely through the great deluge in the Puranic accounts.

questions.q: In many Dashavatara lists, which figure is counted as the ninth avatar of Vishnu, before Kalki?
questions.explanation: In the widely followed list, the Buddha is counted as the ninth avatar, with Kalki as the tenth yet to come.

questions.q: Which serpent is Vishnu most often depicted reclining upon between his cosmic incarnations?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclines upon the cosmic serpent Shesha (Ananta) upon the ocean of milk in the Puranas.

questions.q: Who is the consort of Vishnu, whose incarnations often accompany the Dashavatara?
questions.explanation: Lakshmi, goddess of fortune, is the consort of Vishnu and incarnates alongside him, as Sita with Rama and Rukmini with Krishna.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which divine bird serves as the vahana (mount) of Vishnu across his incarnations?
questions.explanation: Garuda, the divine eagle, is the vahana (mount) of Vishnu across his incarnations in the Puranas.

questions.q: On which mount is the future Kalki avatar of Vishnu prophesied to appear?
questions.explanation: The Puranas prophesy that Kalki will appear riding a white horse and wielding a blazing sword.

questions.q: Which two avatars immediately precede Buddha and Kalki at the close of the classical Dashavatara sequence?
questions.explanation: The classical sequence runs through Rama and Krishna just before reaching Buddha and the future Kalki.

questions.q: Which boar-slain demon does the Varaha avatar of Vishnu defeat while rescuing the earth?
questions.explanation: Varaha slays the demon Hiranyaksha, who had dragged the earth into the cosmic waters, in the Puranic accounts.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana, which avatar is regarded as an incarnation of the serpent Shesha and is sometimes placed among the ten?
questions.explanation: Balarama, elder brother of Krishna, is regarded as an incarnation of the serpent Shesha and appears in some Dashavatara lists in place of Buddha.

questions.q: The progression of the Dashavatara is often described as tracing a movement from the waters to which culmination?
questions.explanation: The sequence is read as a cosmic progression from the aquatic Matsya to the perfected human, reflecting an ascent of forms.

questions.q: The Dashavatara appear, according to tradition, to restore which cosmic principle when it declines?
questions.explanation: The Puranas state that Vishnu descends as the avatars to restore dharma whenever cosmic order falters.

questions.q: In which kind of text is the enumeration of the Dashavatara most fully developed?
questions.explanation: The classical enumeration of the ten avatars is most fully developed in the Puranas, such as the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu is associated with the slaying of the serpent Kaliya in the Yamuna river?
questions.explanation: Krishna subdues the venomous serpent Kaliya in the Yamuna during his youth in Vrindavan, as told in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the Rama avatar of Vishnu is married to which goddess, herself regarded as an incarnation of Lakshmi?
questions.explanation: Rama is married to Sita, the daughter of King Janaka, who is regarded in tradition as an incarnation of Lakshmi.
